content:
Hi,

While going through the code, I noticed that dev_xx! macros are used in many places with manual \n for newlines. Is this intentional, or would it be worth adding variants that include a trailing newline, like how the standard library has both print! and println!?

I tried searching through past discussions but couldn't find anything relevant. Please let me know if I missed them!

Thanks!
It is intentional, yeah. We were asked to keep it close to the C side.
Which in general it is a good idea, especially if the names are exactly the same ones.
Now, one could argue to add ln versions, but there is the downside of introducing twice the number of APIs, and there would likely still be mistakes.
So we ended up with just the same pattern.
I hope that helps explaining the reasoning!

Wow, that's a perspective I hadn't considered! Every additional API comes with added complexity, and if the benefits don't clearly outweigh the costs, the simpler approach is the right call.

This was really helpful, thanks!
Hsiu Che Yu has marked this topic as resolved.
